compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The completion-verification close gate's deterministic referencing-commit precondition (`src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`, `_completion_precheck` / `_referencing_commit_exists`) evaluates a ticket in isolation: it blocks the close of any ticket that records `file_impact` unless a commit references **that ticket's own id** via a `rebar-ticket:` trailer. A parent (epic/story) whose code is delivered by its children's commits therefore cannot close — even though every child landed a referencing commit and closed signed — forcing a `--force-close` that leaves the parent unsigned.

## Reproduction Steps
- [ ] Create an epic with `file_impact` set (a planning hint) and decompose it into children.
- [ ] Implement each child; each commit carries a `rebar-ticket: <child>` trailer (not the epic's id). Close each child (they close signed).
- [ ] `rebar transition <epic> in_progress closed`.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** The precondition credits the parent's entire descendant subtree — if the epic (or any descendant) has a referencing commit, the "work landed" precondition passes, the completion verifier runs on the epic's own criteria, and the epic can close **signed**.
- **Actual:** `Error: cannot close <epic>: it records file_impact (a code change) but no commit references it.` The gate never looks at descendant commits, so the only way to close is `--force-close` (unsigned).

## Design
Evaluate the referencing-commit precondition over the ticket **and its entire descendant subtree** (transitive — an epic is satisfied by grandchild task commits). Build the accepted-id set = `{ticket}` ∪ all descendants (via `rebar._engine_support.descendants.list_descendants`), resolve each, and pass the precondition if **any** commit trailer resolves into that set. `_referencing_commit_exists` takes a set of resolved ids and returns True on the first match.

Soundness: the open-children guard runs first, so a parent only reaches this precondition once all children are closed, and each child already passed this exact referencing-commit check at its own close — so crediting descendants cannot admit unlanded work. Stays deterministic (no LLM).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`: the referencing-commit precondition passes when a `rebar-ticket:` trailer references the ticket **or any of its descendants** (transitive subtree via `list_descendants`); a ticket with `file_impact` and no referencing commit anywhere in its subtree still fails closed.
- [ ] `_referencing_commit_exists` accepts a set of accepted resolved ids and returns True on the first commit whose extracted+resolved ref is in the set (behavior for the single-id case is unchanged).
- [ ] The block message names the subtree (e.g. "no commit references it (nor any of its descendants)").
- [ ] A leaf ticket (no descendants) with `file_impact` and its own referencing commit still closes; a leaf with `file_impact` and no referencing commit still fails closed (no regression).
- [ ] Tests cover: parent with file_impact + a grandchild referencing commit → precondition passes; parent with file_impact + no subtree commit → fails closed; leaf unchanged (both directions).

file_impact.reason: credit descendant subtree in the referencing-commit precondition

compiled_state.title: close gate: credit descendant commits when checking a parent's referencing-commit precondition
